description:
Research GHS hazard classifications for Acetone, Sodium Hydroxide, and Toluene using the NIH PubChem database (https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/). For each chemical, identify the CAS number, GHS Signal Word, GHS Hazard Statements (H-codes), and NFPA 704 diamond ratings (Health, Fire, Reactivity).

Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Chemical Safety Research' containing bookmarks for each chemical's PubChem page. Generate a JSON report at '~/Documents/chemical_hazard_summary.json' using the following structure with lowercase keys:

{
  "acetone": {
    "cas_number": "string",
    "ghs_signal_word": "string",
    "ghs_hazard_statements": ["string"],
    "nfpa_health": number,
    "nfpa_fire": number,
    "nfpa_reactivity": number
  },
  "sodium_hydroxide": {
    "cas_number": "string",
    "ghs_signal_word": "string",
    "ghs_hazard_statements": ["string"],
    "nfpa_health": number,
    "nfpa_fire": number,
    "nfpa_reactivity": number
  },
  "toluene": {
    "cas_number": "string",
    "ghs_signal_word": "string",
    "ghs_hazard_statements": ["string"],
    "nfpa_health": number,
    "nfpa_fire": number,
    "nfpa_reactivity": number
  }
}
